heading: Multiple employer plans and other special rules

children.heading: Plan maintained by more than one employer
children.label: a Plan maintained by more than one employer

children.text: shall be applied as if all employees of each of the employers were employed by a single employer.Section 1052 of this title

Sections 1053 and 1054 of this title shall be applied as if all such employers constituted a single employer, except that the application of any rules with respect to breaks in service shall be made under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.
The minimum funding standard provided by shall be determined as if all participants in the plan were employed by a single employer.section 1082 of this title

children.heading: Maintenance of plan of predecessor employer
children.label: b Maintenance of plan of predecessor employer

children.text: in any case in which the employer maintains a plan of a predecessor employer, service for such predecessor shall be treated as service for the employer, and

in any case in which the employer maintains a plan which is not the plan maintained by a predecessor employer, service for such predecessor shall, to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, be treated as service for the employer.

children.heading: Plan maintained by controlled group of corporations
children.label: c Plan maintained by controlled group of corporations
children.text: For purposes of sections 1052, 1053, and 1054 of this title, all employees of all corporations which are members of a controlled group of corporations (within the meaning of , determined without regard to section 1563(a)(4) and (e)(3)(C) of title 26) shall be treated as employed by a single employer. With respect to a plan adopted by more than one such corporation, the minimum funding standard of shall be determined as if all such employers were a single employer, and allocated to each employer in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury.section 1563(a) of title 26section 1082 of this title

children.heading: Plan of trades or businesses under common control
children.label: d Plan of trades or businesses under common control
children.text: For purposes of sections 1052, 1053, and 1054 of this title, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, all employees of trades or businesses (whether or not incorporated) which are under common control shall be treated as employed by a single employer. The regulations prescribed under this subsection shall be based on principles similar to the principles which apply in the case of subsection (c).

children.heading: Special rules for eligible combined defined benefit plans and qualified cash or deferred arrangements
children.label: e Special rules for eligible combined defined benefit plans and qualified cash or deferred arrangements

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, this chapter shall be applied to any defined benefit plan or applicable individual account plan which are part of an eligible combined plan in the same manner as if each such plan were not a part of the eligible combined plan. In the case of a termination of the defined benefit plan and the applicable defined contribution plan forming part of an eligible combined plan, the plan administrator shall terminate each such plan separately.1So in original. Probably should be “is”.1

children.label: i
children.text: which is maintained by an employer which, at the time the plan is established, is a small employer,

children.label: ii
children.text: which consists of a defined benefit plan and an applicable individual account plan each of which qualifies under ,section 401(a) of title 26

children.label: iii
children.text: the assets of which are held in a single trust forming part of the plan and are clearly identified and allocated to the defined benefit plan and the applicable individual account plan to the extent necessary for the separate application of this chapter under paragraph (1), and

children.label: iv
children.text: with respect to which the benefit, contribution, vesting, and nondiscrimination requirements of subparagraphs (B), (C), (D), (E), and (F) are met.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The benefit requirements of this subparagraph are met with respect to the defined benefit plan forming part of the eligible combined plan if the accrued benefit of each participant derived from employer contributions, when expressed as an annual retirement benefit, is not less than the applicable percentage of the participant’s final average pay. For purposes of this clause, final average pay shall be determined using the period of consecutive years (not exceeding 5) during which the participant had the greatest aggregate compensation from the employer.

children.label: I
children.text: 1 percent multiplied by the number of years of service with the employer, or

children.label: II
children.text: 20 percent.

children.heading: Special rule for applicable defined benefit plans
children.label: iii Special rule for applicable defined benefit plans
children.text: If the defined benefit plan under clause (i) is an applicable defined benefit plan as defined in which meets the interest credit requirements of , the plan shall be treated as meeting the requirements of clause (i) with respect to any plan year if each participant receives pay credit for the year which is not less than the percentage of compensation determined in accordance with the following table:section 1053(f)(3)(B) of this titlesection 1054(b)(5)(B)(i) of this titleIf the participant’s age as of thebeginning of the year is—The percentage is—30 or less2Over 30 but less than 40440 or over but less than 50650 or over8.

children.heading: Years of service
children.label: iv Years of service
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, years of service shall be determined under the rules of paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of , except that the plan may not disregard any year of service because of a participant making, or failing to make, any elective deferral with respect to the qualified cash or deferred arrangement to which subparagraph (C) applies.section 1053(b) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the qualified cash or deferred arrangement included in such plan constitutes an automatic contribution arrangement, and

children.label: II
children.text: the employer is required to make matching contributions on behalf of each employee eligible to participate in the arrangement in an amount equal to 50 percent of the elective contributions of the employee to the extent such elective contributions do not exceed 4 percent of compensation.

children.heading: Nonelective contributions
children.label: ii Nonelective contributions
children.text: An applicable individual account plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of clause (i) because the employer makes nonelective contributions under the plan but such contributions shall not be taken into account in determining whether the requirements of clause (i)(II) are met.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan forming part of an eligible combined plan an employee who has completed at least 3 years of service has a nonforfeitable right to 100 percent of the employee’s accrued benefit under the plan derived from employer contributions, and

children.label: I
children.text: an employee has a nonforfeitable right to any matching contribution made under the qualified cash or deferred arrangement included in such plan by an employer with respect to any elective contribution, including matching contributions in excess of the contributions required under subparagraph (C)(i)(II), and

children.label: II
children.text: an employee who has completed at least 3 years of service has a nonforfeitable right to 100 percent of the employee’s accrued benefit derived under the arrangement from nonelective contributions of the employer.

children.heading: Uniform provision of contributions and benefits
children.label: E Uniform provision of contributions and benefits
children.text: In the case of a defined benefit plan or applicable individual account plan forming part of an eligible combined plan, the requirements of this subparagraph are met if all contributions and benefits under each such plan, and all rights and features under each such plan, must be provided uniformly to all participants.

children.heading: Requirements must be met without taking into account social security and similar contributions and benefits or other plans
children.label: F Requirements must be met without taking into account social security and similar contributions and benefits or other plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if the requirements of clauses (ii) and (iii) are met.

children.heading: Social security and similar contributions
children.label: ii Social security and similar contributions
I
children.text: the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) are met without regard to section 401() of title 26, andl

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements of sections 401(a)(4) and 410(b) of title 26 are met with respect to both the applicable defined contribution plan and defined benefit plan forming part of an eligible combined plan without regard to section 401() of title 26.l

children.heading: Other plans and arrangements
children.label: iii Other plans and arrangements
children.text: The requirements of this clause are met if the applicable defined contribution plan and defined benefit plan forming part of an eligible combined plan meet the requirements of sections 401(a)(4) and 410(b) of title 26 without being combined with any other plan.

children.label: i
children.text: provides that each employee eligible to participate in the arrangement is treated as having elected to have the employer make elective contributions in an amount equal to 4 percent of the employee’s compensation unless the employee specifically elects not to have such contributions made or to have such contributions made at a different rate, and

children.label: ii
children.text: meets the notice requirements under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if the requirements of clauses (ii) and (iii) are met.

children.heading: Reasonable period to make election
children.label: ii Reasonable period to make election
I
children.text: receives a notice explaining the employee’s right under the arrangement to elect not to have elective contributions made on the employee’s behalf or to have the contributions made at a different rate, and

children.label: II
children.text: has a reasonable period of time after receipt of such notice and before the first elective contribution is made to make such election.

children.heading: Annual notice of rights and obligations
children.label: iii Annual notice of rights and obligations
children.text: The requirements of this clause are met if each employee eligible to participate in the arrangement is, within a reasonable period before any year, given notice of the employee’s rights and obligations under the arrangement.

children.heading: Treatment of separate plans
children.label: A Treatment of separate plans
children.text: The except clause in shall not apply to an eligible combined plan.section 1002(35) of this title

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: B Reporting
children.text: An eligible combined plan shall be treated as a single plan for purposes of .section 1023 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “applicable individual account plan” means an individual account plan which includes a qualified cash or deferred arrangement.

children.heading: Qualified cash or deferred arrangement
children.label: B Qualified cash or deferred arrangement
children.text: The term “qualified cash or deferred arrangement” has the meaning given such term by .section 401(k)(2) of title 26

children.heading: Cooperative and small employer charity pension plans
children.label: f Cooperative and small employer charity pension plans
i
children.text: section 104(a)(2) of such Act;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amendments to such section 104 by section 202(b) of the Preservation of Access to Care for Medicare Beneficiaries and Pension Relief Act of 2010; and

children.label: iii
children.text: paragraph (3)(B);

children.label: B
children.text: that, as of , was maintained by more than one employer and all of the employers were organizations described in ;June 25, 2010section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.label: i
children.text: described in section 501(c)(3) of such title,

children.label: ii
children.text: chartered under part B of subtitle II of title 36,

children.label: iii
children.text: with employees in at least 40 States, and

children.label: iv
children.text: whose primary exempt purpose is to provide services with respect to children; or

children.label: i
children.text: described in ,section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.label: ii
children.text: who has been in existence since at least 1938,

children.label: iii
children.text: who conducts medical research directly or indirectly through grant making, and

children.label: iv
children.text: whose primary exempt purpose is to provide services with respect to mothers and children.

children.heading: Aggregation
children.label: 2 Aggregation
children.text: All employers that are treated as a single employer under subsection (b) or (c) of shall be treated as a single employer for purposes of determining if a plan was maintained by more than one employer under subparagraph (B) and (C) of paragraph (1).section 414 of title 262So in original. Probably should be “subparagraphs”.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a plan falls within the definition of a CSEC plan under this subsection (without regard to this paragraph), such plan shall be a CSEC plan unless the plan sponsor elects not later than the close of the first plan year of the plan beginning after , not to be treated as a CSEC plan. An election under the preceding sentence shall take effect for such plan year and, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury.December 31, 2013

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: If a plan described in subparagraph (A) is treated as a CSEC plan, section 104 of the Pension Protection Act of 2006, as amended by the Preservation of Access to Care for Medicare Beneficiaries and Pension Relief Act of 2010, shall cease to apply to such plan as of the first date as of which such plan is treated as a CSEC plan.
